title: Stochastic Path Sampler For Lattice Field Theory
claim: In the reported 2D phi4 benchmark, SPS plus IMH reproduces the reported HMC observables; a cost-matched comparison remains open.
boundary: The autocorrelation units differ between IMH steps and HMC trajectories, so the paper does not establish a cost-matched total-efficiency comparison.
do_not_write: SPS is universally faster than HMC.

title: Flow-based generative models for Markov chain Monte Carlo in lattice field theory
claim: Flow proposals can be coupled to an independence MH correction to retain an exact target chain under the paper's stated conditions.
source_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and Sec. III.
boundary: The concrete numerical demonstration is 2D phi4; higher-dimensional and QCD scaling are explicitly future-development questions.
do_not_write: A learned flow removes critical slowing down in all lattice field theories.

title: Equivariant flow-based sampling for lattice gauge theory
claim: Gauge equivariance can be built into flow proposals rather than learned only from data.
boundary: The stated demonstration is 2D U(1), so it does not by itself prove performance for general gauge groups or QCD.
do_not_write: This result proves the same efficiency gain for all gauge theories.

title: Path Integral Sampler: a stochastic control approach for sampling
claim: PIS is a path-space control neighbor that preserves an importance-weight correction route for an unnormalized target.
boundary: This is a general-sampling paper rather than a lattice-field benchmark, so its relation to SPS is methodological rather than empirical equivalence.
do_not_write: PIS and SPS are the same algorithm.

title: Stochastic normalizing flows as non-equilibrium transformations
claim: Stochastic normalizing flows provide a documented Jarzynski and nonequilibrium bridge in the SPS method family.
boundary: It establishes a conceptual and algorithmic bridge, not a direct SPS-versus-baseline performance comparison.
do_not_write: The paper proves that every nonequilibrium learned sampler has the same performance.

title: Stochastic normalizing flows for lattice field theory
claim: The SNF literature explicitly treats deterministic flow layers and stochastic updates as one hybrid sampler family.
source_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 2.
boundary: This is a short proceeding and should be used as an implementation account, with detailed claims checked against the underlying article.
do_not_write: This short proceeding establishes a general compute advantage.

title: Denoising Diffusion Samplers
claim: DDS is a relevant diffusion and path-measure comparison point for unnormalized targets, not evidence of lattice performance.
source_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
boundary: DDS is an adjacent unnormalized-target method, not a lattice-field result in this paper.
do_not_write: DDS validates SPS on lattice phi4.

title: Diffusion Models as Stochastic Quantization in Lattice Field Theory
claim: This paper is a lattice diffusion and stochastic-quantization predecessor relevant to SPS's Langevin-path framing.
boundary: The claim is tied to the tested 2D phi4 setup and the reported model configuration.
do_not_write: Diffusion sampling is automatically exact without a correction analysis.

title: Out-of-equilibrium simulations to fight topological freezing
claim: Out-of-equilibrium boundary protocols are a nearby route for addressing topological freezing with a correction-like estimator.
source_anchor: PDF p. 1 abstract and p. 2 Sec. 1.
boundary: It is a conference contribution on CP(N-1), and its link to SPS is a shared nonequilibrium family rather than a verified direct root citation.
do_not_write: This is a direct SPS baseline on the same model.

title: Mitigating topological freezing using out-of-equilibrium simulations
claim: This work supplies a concrete nonequilibrium evaluation template beyond ESS alone.
boundary: The article presents a non-ML protocol and says machine-learning combinations are future work; it is not evidence that an SNF or SPS has been tested there.
do_not_write: SPS has already been shown to solve topological freezing by this paper.

title: Variational Autoregressive Networks Applied to $φ^4$ Field Theory Systems
claim: Data-free autoregressive proposals plus MH correction are a direct SPS-family comparison with an explicit residual-bias boundary.
boundary: The evidence is for the reported Ising and phi4 settings and uses ESS alongside corrections; it does not itself establish a universal wall-time win.
do_not_write: VAN eliminates critical slowing down in all regimes.

title: Scalable Generative Sampling and Multilevel Estimation for Lattice Field Theories Near Criticality
claim: Multiscale architecture and MLMC are a current alternative route to critical lattice sampling that needs observable-level cost comparison with SPS.
boundary: The reported numerical claim is a 2D critical-phi4 result and must not be recast as a general cost-normalized theorem.
do_not_write: This proves every learned sampler scales favorably at criticality.

title: Operator Spectroscopy of Trained Lattice Samplers
claim: Operator spectroscopy gives a mechanistic audit that can complement ensemble-level SPS diagnostics.
boundary: Operator spectroscopy is a diagnostic of learned functions; it is not by itself a sampling-efficiency benchmark.
do_not_write: A low projection residual proves a sampler is exact or fast.

title: Diffusion Models for Sampling Near Criticality in Lattice Field Theories
claim: This paper provides a current evaluation template combining observables, local diagnostics, and transfer checks.
boundary: This is a July 2026 forward neighbor of SPS, and its explicit residuals show why a high summary metric alone is insufficient.
do_not_write: The diffusion model is unbiased for every observable and volume.
